body.title: Add created_by to ticket schema; auto-inject igor/claude at source
body.result: added created_by field: queue_task auto-injects 'igor'; cc_queue list shows [igor/claude/unknown] tag; 7 tests passing
body.description:
Tickets have no attribution field — can't tell who filed what. Mystery tickets (T-claude-1, T-nw-1) would have been immediately identifiable as Igor-authored with this in place.

Add `created_by` field to ticket JSON schema in cc_queue.py. In `queue_task()` (ops.py), unconditionally set `created_by: 'igor'` — override any LLM-provided value so attribution is authoritative, not dependent on LLM compliance. CC's /ticket skill sets `created_by: 'claude'`. Display in `cc_queue.py list` output as a small bracketed tag e.g. `[igor]`. Display in `cc_queue.py show` output.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (schema display + list/show rendering), wild_igor/igor/tools/ops.py (queue_task auto-inject, one targeted addition)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; ops.py is MEDIUM inertia — one-line addition to queue_task(), not a refactor; auto-inject at the tool boundary is authoritative over LLM output
**Scope boundary:** In scope: field schema, auto-inject at filing time (queue_task), display in list/show. Out of scope: backfill of existing tickets (leave as null/unknown — display handles gracefully), Scraps reading this field (handled in T-scraps-device).
**Test plan:** test queue_task sets created_by='igor' unconditionally even when input JSON omits it; test cc_queue list shows [igor] tag; test cc_queue show displays created_by; existing tickets with null created_by render as [unknown] without error
